steps.title: Identify the slope and y-intercept
steps.content: The slope-intercept form applies here: $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept. We're given slope = 69. The condition $f(0) = 3$ tells us the y-intercept: when $x = 0$, we have $f(0) = m(0) + b = b = 3$.

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: Substituting $m = 69$ and $b = 3$ into the slope-intercept form gives $f(x) = 69x + 3$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
